paper_title: Mummified Seed Cone Analysis from Maoming Basin

methods.description: Collection of mummified seed cones from the Zhenjiang opencast mine and dating of plant-bearing deposits using accelerator mass spectrometry.

steps.description: The mummified seed cones were collected from the Zhenjiang opencast mine within the Maoming Basin.
steps.searchable_summary: collect mummified seed cones from specified location

steps.description: The plant-bearing deposits were dated to the late Pleistocene using the accelerator mass spectrometry 14 C dating of fossil wood and fruit samples.

methods.description: Preparation of fossil cones for imaging and analysis using various microscopy techniques.

steps.description: The specimens were mechanically cleaned from the rock using needles and brushes.
Fossil cones were photographed using a Panasonic GX7 with a Leica DG MacroElmarit 1:2.8/45 mm macro lens and an Olympus SZX10 stereo microscope with a Tucsen H-694CICE digital camera.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph fossil cones using specified equipment

steps.description: All photographs were optimized for size, color, and contrast using Adobe Photoshop CC 2018.
A micro-CT system was used to study the internal cone structure, specifically a Zeiss 520 Versa 3-D X-ray microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: use micro-CT to study internal structure

steps.description: Transverse breaks of the cone axis and cone scales were mounted on SEM stubs with carbon tape, sputtered with palladium and gold, and examined using a Tescan SEM.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and examine cone samples using SEM

steps.description: Cone scale fragments were prepared using hydrochloric and hydrofluoric acids and treated with KOH.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare cone scale fragments with acid treatment

steps.description: The obtained cone scale cuticles were mounted on aluminum stubs, coated with palladium, and examined using the Tescan SEM at a high vacuum.
steps.searchable_summary: examine cone scale cuticles using SEM
